page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d3s.12xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is disabled, providing dedicated physical execution resources for each virtual CPU.

The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A primary characteristic of this server type is its massive local storage capacity, offering 286044 GB of local hard disk drive (HDD) storage. This high-density storage configuration is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 60 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers.

Given the massive local HDD capacity, the ecs.d3s.12xlarge provides high storage density per resource unit, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for large-scale sequential data access workloads where solid-state drive performance is not a strict requirement. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon cores, high network bandwidth, and massive HDD capacity makes this instance type suitable for large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems, big data analytics, and cold storage database workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d3s.12xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual machine running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. It is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The defining feature of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, offering 286044 GB of local HDD storage. This high-density storage is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 60 Gbps. The ecs.d3s.12xlarge is designed for storage-intensive workloads that require high sequential read and write capabilities, such as distributed file systems, big data analytics, and large-scale data warehousing. It offers a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for massive data storage where solid-state drive performance is not required.
